description_long: This object depicts a formation of six small, stylized airplanes flying in a tight, arrowhead pattern. Each airplane is leaving a long, colored contrail behind it, with three red trails on one side and three blue trails on the other, converging at the front. The airplanes have swept wings and a compact fuselage, with visible cockpit canopies and tail fins. The contrails are straight and vividly colored, emphasizing the sense of coordinated flight and speed. The airplanes appear to be modeled after aerobatic or military jet aircraft, commonly seen in airshows. The overall composition suggests a dynamic, synchronized aerial display.
description: A group of six stylized jet airplanes in tight formation, each leaving vivid red or blue contrails, evoking an aerobatic airshow display.
description_view_0: Side view showing all six airplanes in formation with three red and three blue contrails extending behind them.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full length of the colored contrails and the symmetrical arrangement of the airplanes.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the airplanes facing right, again showing the colored contrails and tight formation.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the noses of the airplanes and the converging pattern of the contrails behind them.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single jet engine per airplane, integrated into the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy per airplane.
